title: wasm: challenge PoW field silently ignored — WASM client cannot connect to PoW-enabled relay
description: wasm/src/client.rs NieRelayClient::connect() extracts only the 'nonce' field from the challenge notification (line 67: v['params']['nonce'].as_str()). The 'difficulty' and 'server_salt' fields from ChallengeParams are never read. When the relay sends difficulty > 0, the WASM client ignores it and sends an authenticate request without a pow_token. The relay rejects this with POW_REQUIRED (-32030). The WASM client then receives an error response to the authenticate request, but send_request() returns Err(String) — the auth_result error is propagated as 'PoW token required' from connect(). No PoW mining is implemented in WASM. A relay with PoW enabled is completely inaccessible to WASM clients. This is not documented as a limitation — a deployer enabling PoW would silently break all browser clients.
